summary: cursed-modules ships install-time recon, AWS credential theft, and a command-relay backdoor
details: On npm install, all three lifecycle hooks (preinstall/install/postinstall) execute install.js, which runs shell reconnaissance (id, find for *flag*, grep for HTB{...} tokens across /), reads candidate flag files, serializes process.env, base64-encodes the bundle, and PUTs it to a remote endpoint. The main module index.js executes a require-time IIFE that, when the host matches a Docker container ID pattern, scrapes AWS environment variables, runs `aws sts get-caller-identity`, lists Secrets Manager entries, and curls the EC2 IMDS endpoint http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/ for IAM role credentials, then PUTs the base64-encoded bundle to http://154.57.164.70:32447. recon.js performs the same host/env collection plus targeted reads of Verdaccio configuration (config.yaml,.verdaccio-db.json, htpasswd) and the installer's.npmrc, exfiltrating to http://154.57.164.76:30728. rce.sh documents a two-way command-relay backdoor against 154.57.164.70:32447: an operator writes shell commands into one slot (ECT-654321) and reads base64-encoded command output from another (ECT-987654), with the install-time and require-time uploaders serving as the victim-side half of the relay. publish-and-arm.sh additionally automates a dependency-confusion attack, publishing the package to public npm and arming a Verdaccio-proxy takeover via a manifest PUT to the same C2 host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
